questions.body: Shown in the figure is a circular loop of radius r and resistance R. A variable magnetic field of induction B = B0e^-t is established inside the coil. If the key (K) is closed, the electrical power developed right after closing the switch is equal to
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
P = e^2/R ; e = d/dt (BA) = A d/dt (B.e^-t) = AB_0 e^-t ⇒ P = 1/R (AB_0 e^-t)^2 = A^2 B_0^2 e^-2t/R
At the time of starting t = 0 so P = A^2 B_0^2/R ⇒ P = (πr^2)^2 B_0^2/R = B_0^2 π^2 r^4/R

questions.body: The masses and radii of the earth and moon are M₁, R₁ and M₂, R₂ respectively. Their centres are distance d apart. The minimum velocity with which a particle of mass m should be projected from a point midway between their centres so that it escapes to infinity is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution: Gravitational potential at mid point V = -GM1/d/2 + -GM2/d/2. Now, PE = m × V = -2Gm/d (M1 + M2) [m = mass of particle]. So, for projecting particle from mid point to infinity KE =| PE|. => 1/2 mv^2 = 2Gm/d (M1 + M2) => v = 2√[G(M1 + M2)/d]

questions.body: In the given circuit the current I₁ is

questions.solution: The circuit can be simplified as follows [Circuit diagram showing resistors and voltage sources]. Applying KCL at junction A: I3 = I1 + I2 .....(i). Applying Kirchoff's voltage law for the loop ABCDA: -30I1 - 40I3 + 40 = 0 => -30I1 - 40(I1 + I2) + 40 = 0 => 7I1 + 4I2 = 4 .....(ii). Applying Kirchoff's voltage law for the loop ADEFA: -40I2 - 40I3 + 80 + 40 = 0 => -40I2 - 40(I1 + I2) = -120 => I1 + 2I2 = 3 ......(iii). On solving equation (ii) and (iii) I1 = -0.4A.

questions.body: When a ceiling fan is switched off its angular velocity reduces to 50% while it makes 36 rotations. How many more rotation will it make before coming to rest (Assume uniform angular retardation)
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
By using equation ω² = ω₀² - 2αθ
(ω₀/2)² = ω₀² - 2α(2π) ⇒ α = 3ω₀²/(4·4π×36), (n = 36)
Now let fan completes total n' revolution from the starting to come to rest
0 = ω₀² - 2α(2πn') ⇒ n' = ω₀²/(4απ)
substituting the value of α from equation (i)
n' = ω₀²·4×4π×36/(4π·3ω₀²) = 48 revolution
Number of rotation = 48 - 36 = 12

questions.body: A flat plate moves normally with a speed v₁ towards a horizontal jet of water of uniform area of cross-section. The jet discharges water at the rate of volume V per second at a speed of v₂. The density of water is ρ. Assume that water splashes along the surface of the plate at right angles to the original motion. The magnitude of the force acting on the plate due to the jet of water is
questions.subject: physics
questions.solution:
Force acting on plate, F = dp/dt = -(dm/dt)
Mass of water reaching the plate per sec = dm/dt = A·v·ρ = A(v₁ + v₂)ρ = V/v₂(v₁ + v₂)ρ
(v = v₁ + v₂ = velocity of water coming out of jet w.r.t. plate) (A = Area of cross section of jet = V/v₂)
∴ F = dm/dt·v = V/v₂(v₁ + v₂)ρ×(v₁ + v₂) = ρ[V/v₂](v₁ + v₂)²

questions.body: Iron forms two oxides, in first oxide 56 gram. Iron is found to be combined with 16 gram oxygen and in second oxide 112 gram. Iron is found to be combined with 48 gram oxygen. This data satisfy the law of
questions.subject: chemistry
questions.solution:
In First oxide : Mass of Fe = 56 gm
Mass of O2 = 16 gm
In Second oxide : Mass of Fe = 112 gm
Mass of O2 = 48 gm
112 gm Fe combines with 48 gm of oxygen
56 gm Fe combines with 48/112 × 56 = 24 gm of oxygen
Thus, the masses of oxygen in two oxides which combines with 56 gm (Fixed amount) of iron are in the ratio of 2 : 3. This is a simple ratio. Hence the data illustrate law of multiple proportion.

questions.body: The figure below shows a triangle ABC with a semicircle on each of its three sides. If AB = 20, AC = 21 and BC = 29, what is the area of the shaded region?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Note how the 'entire' region is comprised of the two full semicircles (with diameters AB and BC respectively) and triangle ABC. Now note how the shaded region (the mickey-mouse-ear shaped region) is formed by subtracting the largest semicircle (with diameter BC) from the entire region. Okay let's start by calculating the area of the entire region. Note that 20:21:29 is very conveniently a Pythagorean triplet, so triangle ABC is a right triangle. This makes our lives easier. Triangle ABC has area (20·21)/2 = 210, and the two full semicircles have area π(20/2)^2 = 100π and π(21/2)^2 = (441/4)π, so the entire region has area 210 + 100π + (441/4)π. Now, the largest semicircle has area π(29/2)^2 = (841/4)π. Hence the desired mickey-mouse-ear shaped shaded area has area 210 + 100π + (441/4)π - (841/4)π = 210 + 100π - (400/4)π = 210 + 100π - 100π = 210.

questions.body: Two parallel chords in a circle have lengths 10 and 14, and the distance between them is 6. The chord parallel to these chords and midway between them is of length √a where a is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let AB be the chord of length 10, CD be the chord of length 14, E be the point on CD such that AE = 6, F be the point on CD such that BF = 6. Then CE = FD = 2. Extend BF intersecting the circle at G and let FG = x. By Power of a Point, 6 × x = 12 × 2 ⟹ x = 4. Since ∠ABC is a right angle and A and G are on the circle, the diameter AG = 10√2 by Pythagorean Theorem, so the radius is 5√2. Let O be the center of the circle, H be the midpoint of CD. Then CH = 7 and ∠OHC is a right angle. Then OH = 1, again by Pythagorean Theorem. The chord midway between AB and CD is a distance of 3 away from each, so 2 away from O. Using the Pythagorean Theorem one more time, half the length of the chord is equal to √46. Then √a = 2√46 ⟹ a = 184.

Alternate: Let JM be the diameter of the circle that is perpendicular to the chords, which we will call AB and CD, and let the intersection points be K and L respectively. Let PQ be the chord of length √a, which hits JM at X. Let x = JK, y = JL, and n = PX. Then by Power of a Point K and L: x(d + 6) = 25 and (x + 6)y = 49. Subtracting one from the other yields y - x = 4. Then x(x + 10) = 25. Also, Power of a Point on X yields n^2 = (x + 3)(x + 7) = x(x + 10) + 21 = 25 + 21 = 46 ⟹ PQ = 2√46 = √184.

questions.body: If 3sinθ + 4cosθ = 5, then the value of sinθ is
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
We have, 3sinθ + 4 cosθ = 5 ...(1)
Squaring on both sides
⇒ 9 sin²θ + 16cos²θ + 24sinθ cosθ = 25
⇒ 16sin²θ + 9cos²θ - 24sinθ cosθ = 0
⇒ (4sinθ - 3cosθ)² = 0
⇒ 4sinθ - 3cosθ = 0 ...(2)
By solving (1) and (2), we get
Sinθ = 3/5

questions.body: If three points (0, 0), (3, sqrt(3)) and (3, λ) form an equilateral triangle, then find the sum of all possible values of λ.
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Let the three points be A(0, 0), B (3, √3) and C(3, λ).
∴ AB = BC = CA [∵ △ABC is an equilateral △]
⇒ AB² = BC² = CA² ⇒ AB² = BC²
⇒ (0 - 3)² + (0 - √3)² = (3 - 3)² + (√3 - λ)²
⇒ 9 + 3 = 0 + 3 - 2λ √3 + λ²
⇒ λ² - 2 √3λ - 9 = 0
⇒ λ (λ - 3√3) + √3(λ - 3√3) = 0
⇒ (λ - 3√3)(λ + √3) = 0
⇒ λ = 3√3 or λ = - √3.

questions.body: When Meena turned 16 years old, her parents gave her a cake with n candles, where n has exactly 16 different positive integer divisors. What is the smallest possible value of n ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: 16 = 2 · 2 · 2 · 2 = 2 · 8 = 4 · 4 = 2 · 2 · 4Evaluating each of these cases, we get 2 · 3 · 5 · 7 = 210 for the first case, 2⁷ · 3 for the second case, 2⁸ · 3³ = 216for the third case, and 2³ · 3 · 5 = 120

questions.body: Each of the series 13 + 15 + 17 + ...... and 14 + 17 + 20 + ..... is continued to 100 terms. Find how many terms are identical between the two series
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
Common terms of the series are 17, 23, 29, ......, 209
a = 17, d = 6, aₙ = 209
a + (n - 1)d = 209
17 + (n - 1)6 = 209
Number of terms = 33.

questions.body: A quadratic equation ax^2 - 2ax + b = 0 has two real solutions. What is the average of the solutions?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: The sum of the solutions is -b/a so if we apply this we get the sum is -2a/(-a) = 2 and since the sum is 2, the average of the 2 solutions is 2/2 = 1 → A

questions.body: Let t be a real number satisfying the equation (1 + t)^2 / (1 + t^2) = 13/37. Then (1 + t)^3 / (1 + t^3) = m/n, where m and n are positive coprime integers. Find m + n.
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Now we have a goal: find 1 - l + l^2 in terms of l alone. From the given equation, we have 37(1 + l)^2 = 12(1 + l^2). Expanding and rearranging gives 12l^2 + 37l + 12 = 0. We wish to relate this to l^2 - l + 1, so to force that, we subtract 49l from both sides to get 12(l^2 - l + 1) = -49l. Therefore l^2 - l + 1 = -49l/12. Substituting this into our desired expression gives 1 + 3l/(-49l/12) = 1 - 36/49 = 13/49. Therefore the answer is 13 + 49 = 62. Alternate: Replace 13/37 with a general N. Note that the given rearranges to N = (1 + l)^2/(1 + l^2) = 1 + 2l/(1 + l^2) = 1 + 2/(l + 1/l) ⟹ l + 1/l = 2/(N-1). Now remark that (1 + l)^3/(1 + l^3) = (1 + l)^3/((1 + l)(l^2 - l + 1)) = (l + 1)^2/(l^2 - l + 1) = 1 + 3l/(l^2 - l + 1) = 1 + 3/(l + 1/l - 1). Hence substituting our expression for l + 1/l yields 1 + 3/(l + 1/l - 1) = 1 + 3/(2/(N-1) - 1) = 1 + 3(N-1)/(3-N) = 2N/(3-N). Plugging in N = 13/37 and simplifying gives a result of 13/49, so the requested answers 13 + 49 = 62

questions.body: Kiana has two older twin brothers. The product of their three ages is 128. What is the sum of their three ages?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: 128 = 2^7. Kiana's brothers could then be 8, and she could be 2⋅2 + 8 + 8 = D, 18.

questions.body: What is the remainder when 3^0 + 3^1 + 3^2 + ....... + 3^2009 is divided by 8 ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Powers of 3 mod 8 cycle as follows: 1, 3, 1, 3, 1, 3, ... Therefore, the required sum is 1005(1) + 1005(3) = 1005(4) ≡ 4 (mod 8) So the remainder is 4 (D).

questions.body: Suppose x > 1 is a real number such that x + 1/x = √22. What is x^2 - 1/x^2 ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: Notice that (x + 1/x)^2 = (x - 1/x)^2 + 4, so x - 1/x = √18. So, we can multiply the two equations to get (x + 1/x)(x - 1/x) = x^2 - 1/x^2 = √18√22 = 6√11.

questions.body: Suppose ABCD is a convex quadrilateral satisfying AB = BC, AC = BD, ∠ABD = 80° and ∠CBD = 20°. What is ∠BCD in degrees?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: First consider the projection D' of D such that ABDD' is a parallelogram. Now, let the foot of the perpendicular from B to AC be E, the intersection of AC and BD be F, and the intersection of BD and D'C be G. Then, ∠BFE = 90° - 30° = 60°. It is easy to find that ∠FCB = 40°. Also ∠CAD' = ∠DAB - ∠EAB = 100° - 40° = 60°. Now realize that △AD'C is isosceles. So it is actually an equilateral triangle. Consequently, ∠GCF = 60° and ∠DGC = 120°. Now realize that △D'DC ≅ △BCD. So △GCD is isosceles so ∠GCD = 30°. So ∠BCD = 40° + 60° + 30° = 130°

questions.body: Let a, b, c and d be real numbers with | a - b | = 2, | b - c | = 3 and | c - d| = 4. What is the sum of all possible values of |a - d| ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
|a - b| = 2 ⟹ a - b = 2, -2
|b - c| = 3 ⟹ b - c = 3, -3
|c - d| = 4 ⟹ c - d = 4, -4
Thus, we can have |a - d| = 9, 5, 3, 1 by testing cases, so the sum of these is 18, or (D)

questions.body: The smallest positive integer greater than 1 that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 4, 5, and 6 lies between which of the following pairs of numbers?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution: lcm(4, 5, 6) = 60. Add 1 to get 61, so the answer is D

questions.body: A rectangular floor measures a by b feet, where a and b are positive integers with b > a. An artist paints a rectangle on the floor with the sides of the rectangle parallel to the sides of the floor. The unpainted part of the floor forms a border of width 1 foot around the painted rectangle and occupies half of the area of the entire floor. How many possibilities are there for the ordered pair (a, b) ?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
This inner rectangle has dimensions b - 2 and a - 2. 2 times the area of this rectangle is the area of the larger rectangle with dimensions a and b, so:
2(a - 2)(b - 2) = ab
ab - 4a - 4b + 16 = 8
(a - 4)(b - 4) = 8
8 can be broken up as 1*8 and 2*4. Both give one solution for (a,b) since b > a. Thus, the answer is B) 2

questions.body: Suppose a, b and c are nonzero real numbers such that bc + 1/a = ca + 2/b = ab + 7/c = 1/(a+b+c). Find a + b + c.
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
We have bc + 1/a = a/(a+b+c) ⟹ abc + 1 = a/(a+b+c).
Performing similar operations yields 3abc + 10 = 1 ⟹ abc = -3.
Next, we multiply the three equations together, yielding
(abc)^2 + 10abc + 23 + 1/4/abc = 1/(a+b+c)^3.
It follows that 9 - 30 + 23 - 1/4/3 = 1/(a+b+c)^3, so our answer is -3^(1/3)/2

questions.body: Twenty percent less than 60 is one-third more than what number?
questions.subject: maths

questions.body: The least common multiple of a and b is 12, and the least common multiple of b and c is 15. What is the least possible value of the least common multiple of a and c?
questions.subject: maths
questions.solution:
GCD(12, 15) = 3. So b = 3. Divide both LCM's by 3 to find b and c. We get c as 5 and a as 4. Therefore our LCM is 20
Alternate:
lcm(a, b) = 2^2 · 3
lcm(b, c) = 3 · 5
Therefore, lcm(a, c) = 2^2 · 5 = 20 → A
